items.title: Standard algorithms: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CPP misconception? std::remove_if directly changes a vector's size and destroys every removed element.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. remove_if rearranges and returns a new logical end; use the container's erase operation to reduce its size.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. remove_if rearranges and returns a new logical end; use the container's erase operation to reduce its size.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned CPP correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: remove_if rearranges and returns a new logical end; use the container's erase operation to reduce its size.
std::remove_if directly changes a vector's size and destroys every removed element.

answer.explanation: remove_if rearranges and returns a new logical end; use the container's erase operation to reduce its size.

items.title: Standard algorithms: complete the language rule
items.prompt: Complete the missing CPP checkpoint in the source-aligned correction. Use the exact token or phrase required by this draft.
hints: Use the checkpoint that distinguishes the valid rule from this misconception: std::remove_if directly changes a vector's size and destroys every removed element.

feedback.correct: Correct. The required checkpoint is 'logical end'.
feedback.incorrect: Recheck the exact language rule and its boundary. remove_if rearranges and returns a new logical end; use the container's erase operation to reduce its size.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact CPP token or phrase that completes the reviewed rule candidate.

items.title: Standard algorithms: executable application
items.prompt: Read up to 100 integers into a vector, use standard algorithms to sort them and remove duplicates, then print the unique ascending values separated by one space.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

hints: Start from input validation and the declared observable output, then apply only the target language rule before considering optimization.

feedback.correct: All visible and hidden deterministic behavior checks passed for the bounded source task.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic behavior check failed; inspect bounds, input state, and the target rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every normal and boundary behavior test.
Applies Standard algorithms without undefined behavior or an out-of-scope workaround.

privateAuthorNotes: AI-assisted and human-unreviewed: formal exam eligibility is prohibited until independent technical and assessment approval.
Reference verification uses the digest-pinned local container with network=none, no package installation, a read-only source mount, and deterministic stdin/stdout.
Executable facet: existing bounded executable application retained from the authored language tranche

answer.explanation: The reference solution exercises existing bounded executable application retained from the authored language tranche under the bounded deterministic contract; it remains an AI-assisted draft pending human review.
